# Team News: Norway vs. France injury, suspension list, predicted XIs ahead of World Cup clash

By Freddie Cotton| 25 Jun 2026 20:00, Last updated: 25 Jun 2026 20:00

© Imago / Passion2Press

Norway and France meet at the Gillette Stadium in Foxborough on Friday evening for their final group stage matches in this summer's World Cup.

The Vikings made it two wins from two in Group I when claiming a 3-2 victory against Senegal on Tuesday morning, while Les Bleus achieved the same feat by overcoming Iraq 3-0 in Philadelphia.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both teams ahead of Friday's crucial clash.

---

## Norway vs. France

## Norway

Out: None

Doubtful: Julian Ryerson(thigh)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Nyland; Pedersen, Ajer, Lysaker Heggem, Wolfe; Berge, Aursnes, Odegaard; Sorloth, Haaland, Nusa

## France

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Maignan; Kounde, Upamecano, Saliba; Digne; Tchouameni, Rabiot; Olise, Dembele, Doue; Mbappe

---

World Cup 2026

news_items.text:
Norway vs France XIs: Confirmed team news, predicted lineups and injury latest for World Cup 2026 | The Standard

Sport| Football

# Norway vs France XIs: Confirmed team news, predicted lineups and injury latest for World Cup 2026

Two of world’s best strikers go head-to-head in Golden Boot battle

Oli Gent, Sports Reporter@oli_gent

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland will be hoping to overtake Lionel Messi as the top goalscorer at World Cup 2026 when the pair lock horns tomorrow.

France and Norway face off as Group I reaches its conclusion.

Les Bleus top the table by a single goal, with both sides taking two wins from their opening two matches of the tournament.

Didier Deschamps has plumped for a mostly settled lineup, and he made three changes to his side for the 3-0 win over Iraq, which followed the 3-1 victory against Senegal.

Manu Kone partnered Adrien Rabiot in midfield, with Aurelien Tchouameni dropping to the bench, and Bradley Barcola was given the nod over Desire Doue on the left flank. Lucas Digne came on for Theo Hernandez at left-back.

Those decisions could be reversed against Norway, as the French look to win the group and earn themselves a

news_items.title: France keen to secure top spot and avoid travel disruption, Stephan says | The Straits Times
news_items.text:
France keen to secure top spot and avoid travel disruption, Stephan says | The Straits Times

myST+

Menu

# France keen to secure top spot and avoid travel disruption, Stephan says

Sign up now: Get the biggest sports news in your inbox

Topping the group would mean France would be able to stay in Northeastern United States until the quarter-finals.

PHOTO: REUTERS

Published Jun 26, 2026, 03:46 AM

Updated Jun 26, 2026, 06:53 AM

Set as preferred source

Listen

BOSTON - France will be aiming to secure top spot in their World Cup Group I on June 26 when they take on equally in-form Norway to avoid the disruption of having to travel far from their base in Boston, assistant coach Guy Stephan said.

Speaking on June 25 in place of the team’s World Cup-winning manager Didier Deschamps, who has returned to France for his mother’s funeral, Stephan said central defender William Saliba would be missing against Norway and striker Marcus Thuram was suffering with calf pain.

“First and foremost, we have qualified and it doesn’t happen very often that after two games we have six points,” Stephan told reporters on June 25. “Didier wants to finish top, as the whole group does, because it woul

# Team News: Norway vs. France injury, suspension list, predicted XIs ahead of World Cup clash

By Freddie Cotton| 25 Jun 2026 20:00, Last updated: 25 Jun 2026 20:00

© Imago / Passion2Press

Norway and France meet at the Gillette Stadium in Foxborough on Friday evening for their final group stage matches in this summer's World Cup.

The Vikings made it two wins from two in Group I when claiming a 3-2 victory against Senegal on Tuesday morning, while Les Bleus achieved the same feat by overcoming Iraq 3-0 in Philadelphia.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both teams ahead of Friday's crucial clash.

---

## Norway vs. France

## Norway

Out: None

Doubtful: Julian Ryerson(thigh)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Nyland; Pedersen, Ajer, Lysaker Heggem, Wolfe; Berge, Aursnes, Odegaard; Sorloth, Haaland, Nusa

## France

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Maignan; Kounde, Upamecano, Saliba; Digne; Tchouameni, Rabiot; Olise, Dembele, Doue; Mbappe

---

Sport| Football

# Norway vs France XIs: Confirmed team news, predicted lineups and injury latest for World Cup 2026

Two of world’s best strikers go head-to-head in Golden Boot battle

Oli Gent, Sports Reporter@oli_gent

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland will be hoping to overtake Lionel Messi as the top goalscorer at World Cup 2026 when the pair lock horns tomorrow.

France and Norway face off as Group I reaches its conclusion.

Les Bleus top the table by a single goal, with both sides taking two wins from their opening two matches of the tournament.

Didier Deschamps has plumped for a mostly settled lineup, and he made three changes to his side for the 3-0 win over Iraq, which followed the 3-1 victory against Senegal.

Manu Kone partnered Adrien Rabiot in midfield, with Aurelien Tchouameni dropping to the bench, and Bradley Barcola was given the nod over Desire Doue on the left flank. Lucas Digne came on for Theo Hernandez at left-back.

Those decisions could be reversed against Norway, as the French look to win the group and earn themselves a "}

# France keen to secure top spot and avoid travel disruption, Stephan says

Sign up now: Get the biggest sports news in your inbox

Topping the group would mean France would be able to stay in Northeastern United States until the quarter-finals.

PHOTO: REUTERS

Published Jun 26, 2026, 03:46 AM

Updated Jun 26, 2026, 06:53 AM

Set as preferred source

Listen

BOSTON - France will be aiming to secure top spot in their World Cup Group I on June 26 when they take on equally in-form Norway to avoid the disruption of having to travel far from their base in Boston, assistant coach Guy Stephan said.

Speaking on June 25 in place of the team’s World Cup-winning manager Didier Deschamps, who has returned to France for his mother’s funeral, Stephan said central defender William Saliba would be missing against Norway and striker Marcus Thuram was suffering with calf pain.

“First and foremost, we have qualified and it doesn’t happen very often that after two games we have six points,” Stephan told reporters on June 25. “Didier wants to finish top, as the whole group does, because it woul"}
